title: Shaggy Lumpia
description: Savory lumpia with a twist of sweetness and crunch, perfect for a date night in.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a wok over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the onion and cook for 2 minutes, until translucent.
Add the cabbage and cook for 2 minutes, until slightly softened.
Add the ground pork and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink, about 5 minutes.
Add the shrimp and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are pink, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the caster sugar, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the spring onions and stir to combine.
If using panko breadcrumbs, sprinkle over the top and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, lay a lumpia wrapper on a flat surface and place 1-2 tablespoons of the mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with a little water and fold the bottom half over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the lumpia and fry until they are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Drain the lumpia on paper towels and serve hot.
Repeat the frying process with the remaining lumpia, in batches, if necessary.
Serve the shaggy lumpia hot with a dipping sauce of your choice, such as a sweet chili sauce or a peanut sauce.
Serve immediately.
Enjoy!
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
